title: Double Fried Italian Seafood Fried Rice
description: A pungent and silky Italian seafood dish featuring chicken stock concentrate, double-fry technique, and pescatarian-friendly ingredients, inspired by Venetian cuisine.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the shrimp, scallops, mussels, and clams, and cook for 2-3 minutes until the seafood is lightly browned.
Add the white wine and cook for 1 minute until the liquid is almost completely evaporated.
Add the chicken stock concentrate and cook for 1 minute until the mixture is well combined.
Add the Arborio rice to the skillet, stirring to combine with the seafood mixture.
Cook for 2 minutes until the rice is lightly toasted.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Heat about 1/2 inch of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350 degrees.
Add the rice mixture to the skillet, pressing it down gently with a spatula to form a flat layer.
Cook for 2-3 minutes until the bottom of the rice is crispy and golden brown.
Loosen the rice with a spatula and stir in the chopped parsley.
